title: WordPress REST Posts CRUD Flow

permission_logic: check_is_post_type_allowed($post_type) — returns WP_Error if not REST-enabled
If post_status param requires elevated privileges → current_user_can('edit_posts')
flow_steps: Build query args array from $request params
Call prepare_items_query($prepared_args, $request) → returns sanitized WP_Query args
Loop posts → check_read_permission($post) per post → skip if false
Return WP_REST_Response with posts array + pagination headers

permission_logic: get_post($id) — fetch WP_Post or return WP_Error 404
If post has password AND request has password param → can_access_password_content($post, $request)
If 'status' param set → handle_status_param() checks publish/private caps
flow_steps: wp_insert_post($post_data, true) → returns post_id or WP_Error
handle_terms($post_id, $request) → set taxonomy terms if provided

permission_logic: get_post($id) → 404 if not found or wrong post_type
If changing status → handle_status_param() checks publish/private caps

flow_steps: Return 200 WP_REST_Response with previous post data

shared_helpers: get_post($id): protected — fetches WP_Post, validates post_type match
check_read_permission($post): public — evaluates post_status + user caps
prepare_items_query($prepared_args, $request): protected — sanitizes WP_Query args (orderby, per_page, offset, search, author, exclude, include, sticky, tax_query)
prepare_item_for_database($request): protected — maps REST params to wp_insert/update_post array
prepare_date_response($date_gmt, $date): protected — normalizes date fields for response
check_assign_terms_permission($request): protected — cap check before term assignment
check_is_post_type_allowed($post_type): protected — confirms post type is REST-enabled
check_template($template, $request): public — validates page template exists
branching_conditions: post_status = 'publish' → no auth required for read
force=true on delete → permanent delete vs trash
Post type not REST-enabled → WP_Error returned early
error_codes: rest_invalid_post — post not found or wrong type
key_relationships: WP_REST_Posts_Controller uses WP_REST_Post_Meta_Fields ($meta) for custom meta
WP_REST_Posts_Controller delegates DB ops to WordPress core (wp_insert_post, WP_Query)
important_notes: Each CRUD operation follows a strict two-phase pattern: (1) *_permissions_check() gate evaluated by WordPress before routing, (2) main handler method. Password-protected posts require explicit password in request or editor-level cap. prepare_items_query() maps REST collection params to WP_Query args including tax_query, date_query, meta_query. The controller is instantiated per post_type, supporting custom post types via register_routes().
